label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about figuring out the date one week ago from today, given that Jane booked a flight for tomorrow, which is July 29, 2002. So, first, I need to determine what today's date is based on that information. If the flight is tomorrow, then today must be July 28, 2002. Now, the problem is asking for the date one week ago from today. So, if today is July 28, 2002, subtracting one week (which is 7 days) would take us back to July 21, 2002. Let me double-check that. July has 31 days, right? So July 28 minus 7 days: 28 - 7 = 21. So that would be July 21, 2002. Looking at the options provided, option E is 07/21/2002. Let me make sure I didn't make any mistakes here. If Jane's flight is on July 29 (tomorrow), then today is July 28. Subtracting 7 days from July 28 would be July 21. Yes, that seems correct. Let me check if there's a possibility of a leap year or something affecting the dates, but 2002 isn't a leap year. Leap years are divisible by 4, and 2002 isn't. So February had 28 days. But since we're in July, that doesn't affect this calculation. Another thing to consider: when they say "one week ago from today," does that mean exactly 7 days prior? For example, if today is the 28th, then a week ago would be the 21st. Yes,
